title: The Ukrainian family torn by war and exile behind a famous photo published by Le Monde
subhead: A few days after the Russian invasion, photojournalist Laurence Geai captured the farewell of a Ukrainian family at Odesa train station. Four years later, the mother welcomed Le Monde to Cologne, Germany, where she lives as a refugee with her son and her mother, far from her husband, since enlisted in the army.

subhead: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Sunday that details of a possible deal to forestall fresh conflict were being drawn up ahead of Thursday's renewed talks on Tehran's nuclear program.

title: India, world's leading dairy producer, struggles to rid market of adulterated milk
subhead: Water is often added to Indian milk, but so are chemicals and vegetable oils. The quality of Indian dairy is frequently questionable – a fact highlighted by a recent scandal in the state of Gujarat.

title: Is it ever a good idea to... wear artificially distressed clothing?
subhead: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or else, to do so deliberately.

title: AI has arrived in France's local elections – for better or worse
subhead: From creating campaign videos to writing speeches, artificial intelligence has become an effective tool for candidates. However, it also raises ethical questions, prompting some elected officials to call for regulation.

subhead: The Belarusian woman met the sexual predator when she was 20 and remained loyal to him until his suicide in prison in 2019.

title: Documents show Epstein's attempts at connecting with Sarkozy's circle
subhead: Nicolas Princen, an adviser to Nicolas Sarkozy at the Elysée Palace, met the sex offender in his Paris apartment in 2011. The meeting, which 'had no follow-up,' according to Princen, illustrates Epstein's attempts to approach the French presidency under Sarkozy.

title: Serge Gainsbourg's forgotten Ukrainian roots
subhead: Serge Gainsbourg described himself as a Russian Jew. More precisely, his musician parents fled Tsarist Ukraine before emigrating to Paris in 1921, to escape from pogroms and the Bolsheviks. In Ukraine, where four years of war have fueled an obsession with uncovering traces of the past, some have been trying to rediscover the singer's roots.
